MetaData.Name: [IN PROGRESS] HL7 Version 2.5.1 Implementation Guide: Syndromic Surveillance, Release 1 - US Realm Standard for Trial Use, July 2019

ValueElement.DisplayName: Drug Furnishing or prescriptive authority Number
Medicare/CMS (formerly HCFA)'s Universal Physician Identification numbers
National Person Identifier where the xxx is the ISO table 3166 3-character (alphabetic) country code

Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 37
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 15

Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 33
Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 35
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 10
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 21
Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 34
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 24
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 18
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 27
Expired (i.e. died) in a medical facility; e.g., hospital, SNF, ICF, or free standing hospice
Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 31
Discharged to home or self care (routine discharge)
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 11
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 13
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 26
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 28
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary

Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 29
Discharged/transferred to another short term general hospital for inpatient care
Left against medical advice or discontinued care
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 14
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 16
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 23
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 22
Admitted as an inpatient to this hospital
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary
Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 38
Discharged/transferred to home under care of Home IV provider
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 12
Expired to be defined at state level, if necessary 25
Discharged/transferred to home under care of organized home health service organization
Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 39
Still patient or expected to return for outpatient services (i.e. still a patient)

Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient)
Discharge to be defined at state level, if necessary 17
Still patient to be defined at state level, if necessary (i.e. still a patient) 36
International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM)

Clinicians are required to track the Vaccine Information Sheet (VIS) that was shared with the recipient of a vaccination. This code system contains codes that identify the document type and the owner of the document.
WHO record # code with ASTM extension
Action Taken In Response To The Event
ISBT 128 codes where nnnn specifies a specific table within ISBT 128.
UCUM code set for units of measure(from Regenstrief)
Medical Dictionary for Drug Regulatory Affairs (MEDDRA)
Health Care Provider Area Of Specialization
NCBI - genomic and chromosome reference sequences
International Classification of Diseases for Oncology
ISO 2955.83 (units of measure) with HL7 extensions
Table of HL7 Version 3 ActCode values
Time Selection Criteria Parameter Class Codes
American College of Radiology finding codes
CDC PHLIP Lab test codes, where LOINC concept is too broad or not yet available, especially as needed for ordering and or lab to lab reporting )
International Classification of Functioning, Disability and Health (ICF)
CDC PHLIP Lab result codes that are not covered in SNOMED at the time of this implementation
ISO 2955.83 (units of measure) with HL7 extensions
NCPDP code list for data element nnnn [as used in segment sss]
CDC Public Health Information Network (PHIN) Question
IHE Laboratory Analytical Workflow (LAW) Profile Codes.
ISO Defined Codes where nnnn is the ISO table number. (deprecated)
CMS (formerly HCFA) Common Procedure Coding System

Local general code for a site-defined code system used for a specific set of trading partners. The 'zzz' SHALL be any printable ASCII string. Length of the name SHALL not exceed field width, and is subject to local implementation.
College of American Pathologists Electronic Cancer Checklist
International Classification of Disease for Oncology Third Edition
Logical Observation Identifier Names and Codes (LOINC®)
U.S. Board on Geographic Names (USGS - GNIS)
International Classification of Disease for Oncology Second Edition
International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature (ISCN)
HL7 set of units of measure
Specifies the mode, immediate versus deferred or queued, by which a receiver should communicate its receiver responsibilities.

Local general code for a site-defined code system used for a specific set of trading partners. The 'zzz' SHALL be any printable ASCII string. Length of the name SHALL not exceed field width, and is subject to local implementation.
CDC National Healthcare Safety Network Codes
International Classification of Diseases, 9th Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-9-CM)
International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision, Procedure Coding System (ICD-10-PCS)

Used to indicate that the target of the relationship will be a filtered subset of the total related set of targets. Used when there is a need to limit the number of components to the first, the last, the next, the total, the average or some other filtere
X.400 email address: Use Only If Telecommunication Use Code Is NET
Internet Address: Use Only If Telecommunication Use Code Is NET
The visit is marked for deletion and the user cannot enter new data against it.
The visit is marked inactive and the user cannot enter new data against it.
Marked for purge. User is no longer able to update the visit.
technology fee for use of equipment
unit price, may be based on length of procedure or service
Not present (the default, meaning current processing)
Current processing, transmitted at intervals (scheduled or on demand)
Pro-rate. Apply this price to this interval, pro-rated by whatever portion of the interval has occurred/been consumed
Flat-rate. Apply the entire price to this interval, do not pro-rate the price if the full interval has not occurred/been consumed
Leave of absence (e.g. Family leave, sabbatical, etc.)
Check digit algorithm in the US National Provider Identifier
ADT/ACK - Change alternate patient ID (for backward compatibility only)
QRY/DSR - Query sent for immediate response
MDM/ACK - Document addendum notification and content
SIU/ACK - Notification of new appointment booking
ORU - Unsolicited Point-Of-Care Observation Message Without Existing Order - Place An Order
MFQ/MFR - Master files query (use event same as asking for e.g., M05 - location)
CSU - Automated time intervals for reporting, like monthly
RQD/RPI - Request for patient demographic data
TCR/ACK - Automated equipment test code settings request
ADT/ACK - Merge person - patient ID (for backward compatibility only)
RQI/RPR - Request/receipt of patient selection list
CRM - Cancel a patient registration on clinical trial (for clerical mistakes only)
MFN/MFK - Master file - test/observation (for backward compatibility only)
ORM - Order message (also RDE, RDS, RGV, RAS)
PEX - Unsolicited initial individual product experience report
SRM/SRR - Request addition of service/resource on appointment
DFT/ACK - Post Detail Financial Transactions - New
OUL - Unsolicited Order Oriented Observation Message
ADT/ACK - Merge patient information - account number only (for backward compatibility only)
ADT/ACK - Change patient ID (for backward compatibility only)
ADT/ACK - Merge account - patient account number
ORU - Waveform result, unsolicited transmission of requested information
DSR/ACK - Deferred response to a query
ORU/ACK - Unsolicited transmission of an observation message
ADT/ACK - Move visit information - visit number
RSP - Segment pattern response in response to QBP^Q11
ADT/ACK - Change an inpatient to an outpatient
SRM/SRR - Request deletion of service/resource on appointment
ADT/ACK - Merge patient information - patient ID and account number (for backward compatibility only)
QBP - Query by parameter requesting an RTB - tabular response
SIU/ACK - Notification of addition of service/resource on appointment
ADT/ACK - Merge patient information (for backward compatibility only)
ROR - Pharmacy prescription order query response
ADT/ACK - Patient goes on a "leave of absence"
CSU - Patient completes the clinical trial
MFN/MFK - Clinical study without phases but with schedules master file
QRF - Waveform result, response to query
ADT/ACK - Change an outpatient to an inpatient
ORF - Response to query; transmission of requested observation
QRY/DSR Display-oriented results, query/unsol. update (for backward compatibility only) (Replaced by Q05)
QBP - Query by parameter requesting an RSP segment pattern response
QRY/QCK - Query sent for deferred response
RDY - Display response in response to QBP^Q15
ORL - General laboratory order response message to any OML
CRM - Register a patient on a clinical trial
RTB - Tabular response in response to QBP^Q13
QBP - Query by parameter requesting an RDY display response
ADT/ACK - Move account information - patient account number
SIU/ACK - Notification of cancellation of service/resource on appointment
SRM/SRR - Request modification of service/resource on appointment
SRM/SRR - Request cancellation of service/resource on appointment
BRP - Blood product dispense status acknowledgment
SIU/ACK - Notification of blocked schedule time slot(s)
CRM - Patient has gone off phase of clinical trial
CRM - Patient has gone off a clinical trial
TCU/ACK - Automated equipment test code settings update
ORL - Laboratory order response message to a single container of a specimen OML
CRM - Patient enters phase of clinical trial
QRY - Query for results of observation
MDM/ACK - Document replacement notification and content
ADT/ACK - Move patient information - patient identifier list
RQI/RPL - Request/receipt of patient selection display list
MDM/ACK - Original document notification and content
MFN/MFK - Master file not otherwise specified (for backward compatibility only)
ADT/ACK - Merge patient - patient identifier list
SIU/ACK - Notification of discontinuation of service/resource on appointment
OML - Laboratory order for multiple orders related to a single container of a specimen
VXX - Response to vaccination query returning multiple PID matches
RQA/RPA - Request for cancellation of an authorization
SRM/SRR - Request discontinuation of service/resource on appointment
CSU - Update/correction of patient order/result information
RQC/RCI - Request for patient clinical information
RSP - Personnel Information by Segment Response
ORI - Imaging order response message to any OMI
RQA/RPA - Request for modification to an authorization
MFN/MFK - Clinical study with phases and schedules master file
RQC/RCL - Request/receipt of clinical data listing
SIU/ACK - Notification of deletion of service/resource on appointment
MDM/ACK - Document edit notification and content
QRY/DSP - Generate bill and A/R statements
RQA/RPA - Request for resubmission of an authorization
ADT/ACK - Patient returns from a "leave of absence"
SIU/ACK - Notification of modification of service/resource on appointment
MFN/MFK - Master file notification - site defined
MFN/MFK - Inventory item master file notification
PEX - Unsolicited update individual product experience report
MDM/ACK - Document status change notification and content
OML - Laboratory order for multiple orders related to a single specimen
SIU/ACK - Notification of opened ("unblocked") schedule time slot(s)
ADT/ACK - Cancel patient returns from a leave of absence
CRM - Cancel patient entering a phase (clerical mistake)
QBP - Personnel Information by Segment Query
SQM/SQR - Schedule query message and response
ADT/ACK - Cancel leave of absence for a patient
CSU - Patient completes a phase of the clinical trial
ORU - Unsolicited New Point-Of-Care Observation Message - Search For An Order
ORR - Order response (also RRE, RRD, RRG, RRA)
ADT/ACK - Merge person information (for backward compatibility only)
OUL - Unsolicited Specimen Oriented Observation Message
RQA/RPA - Request for treatment authorization information
ADT/ACK - Merge patient information - patient ID only (for backward compatibility only)
ORL - Laboratory order response message to a multiple order related to single specimen OML
OUL - Unsolicited Specimen Container Oriented Observation Message
SIU/ACK Notification that patient did not show up for schedule appointment
No range defined, or normal ranges don't apply
Resistant. Indicates for microbiology susceptibilities only.
Very susceptible. Indicates for microbiology susceptibilities only.
Very abnormal (applies to non-numeric units, analogous to panic limits for numeric units)
Intermediate. Indicates for microbiology susceptibilities only.
Susceptible. Indicates for microbiology susceptibilities only.
Moderately susceptible. Indicates for microbiology susceptibilities only.
No, patient does not have a living will but information was provided
Yes, patient has a living will but it is not on file
No, patient does not have a living will and no information was provided
Yes, patient has a living will
Inactive - Moved or gone elsewhere (cancel contract)
Inactive - Permanently inactive (Do not reactivate or add new entries to the record)
Signed authorization for assignment of benefits on file.
Signature generated by provider because the patient was not physically present for services.
Signed authorization for release of any medical or other information necessary to process this claim on file.
Signed CMS-1500 claim form on file, e.g. authorization for release of any medical or other information necessary to process this claim and assignment of benefits.
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/1 Character set
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/5 Character set
Code for Korean Character Set (KS X 1001)
Code for Information Exchange (one byte)(JIS X 0201-1976).
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/8 Character set
Code for the Japanese Graphic Character set for information interchange (JIS X 0208-1990),
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/6 Character set
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/7 Character set
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/3 Character set
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/2 Character set
The world wide character standard from ISO/IEC 10646-1-1993
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/9 Character set
Code of the supplementary Japanese Graphic Character set for information interchange (JIS X 0212-1990).
The printable characters from the ISO 8859/4 Character set
Name of Partner/Spouse (retained for backward compatibility only)
No, patient is not a documented donor, but information was provided
Yes, patient is a documented donor, but documentation is not on file
Yes, patient is a documented donor and documentation is on file
Patient leaves organ donation decision to a specific person
Patient leaves organ donation decision to relatives
No, patient has not agreed to be a donor
Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander
Birth delivery location (address where birth occurred)

Registry home. Refers to the information system, typically managed by a public health agency, that stores patient information such as immunization histories or cancer data, regardless of where the patient obtains services.
Residence at birth (home address at time of birth)
Minor dependent of a minor dependent
Natural child/Insured does not have financial responsibility
Christian: Church of God in Christ
Christian: Reorganized Church of Jesus Christ-LDS
Invasive procedure not classified elsewhere (e.g., IV, catheter, etc.)
This is the default, indicating that there is no character set switching occurring in this message.
This standard is titled "Information Technology - Character Code Structure and Extension Technique". .
The character set switching mode specified in HL7 2.5, section 2.7.2, â€œEscape sequences supporting multiple character setsâ€ and section 2.A.46, "XPN - extended person name".
Invasive procedure not classified elsewhere (I.V., catheter, etc.)
Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander
Enhanced mode: Accept acknowledgment: Commit Reject
Enhanced mode: Accept acknowledgment: Commit Accept
Original mode: Application Reject - Enhanced mode: Application acknowledgment: Reject
Enhanced mode: Accept acknowledgment: Commit Error
Original mode: Application Error - Enhanced mode: Application acknowledgment: Error
Original mode: Application Accept - Enhanced mode: Application acknowledgment: Accept
SOUTH GEORGIA AND THE SOUTH SANDWICH ISLANDS
UNITED KINGDOM OF GREAT BRITAIN AND NORTHERN IRELAND (THE)
SAINT HELENA, ASCENSION AND TRISTAN DA CUNHA
The CEN Healthcare Coding Scheme Designator. (Identifiers used in DICOM follow this assignment scheme.)
These are reserved for locally defined coding schemes.
Usually a base64 encoded string of random bits.
These are reserved for locally defined coding schemes.
Reserved for future HL7 registration schemes
These are reserved for locally defined coding schemes.
An International Standards Organization Object Identifier
An Internet dotted name. Either in ASCII or as integers
Medicare/CMS (formerly HCFA)_s Universal Physician Identification numbers
National Person Identifier where the xxx is the ISO table 3166 3-character (alphabetic) country code
Drug Furnishing or prescriptive authority Number
Post original as wrong, e.g., transmitted for wrong patient
Record coming over is a correction and thus replaces a final result
Results cannot be obtained for this observation
Not asked; used to affirmatively document that the observation identified in the OBX was not sought when the universal service ID in OBR-4 implies that it would be sought.
Results status change to final without retransmitting results already sent as 'preliminary.' E.g., radiology changes status from preliminary to final
Final results; Can only be changed with a corrected result.
Long Term Care Facility General Nursing Unit
Long Term Acute Care Pediatric Intensive Care Unit
Long Term Acute Care Pediatric Ward
Oncology Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant Ward
Long Term Care Facility Inpatient Hospice Unit
Oncology Pediatric Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant Ward
Long Term Care Facility Dementia Unit
Long Term Care Facility Skilled Nursing-Short Term Rehabilitation Unit
Pediatric Solid Organ Transplant Specialty Care Area
Long Term Acute Care Intensive Care Unit
Rehabilitation Pediatric Ward (freestanding Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility)
Long Term Care Facility Ventilator Dependent Unit
Outpatient Hemodialysis Clinic - Acute Kidney Injury
Pediatric Rehabilitation Ward (within Acute Care Hospital)
Long Term Care Facility Bariatric Unit
Solid Organ Transplant Specialty Care Area
Long Term Care Facility Psychiatric Unit

Transfer from a skilled nursing facility
Transfer from another health care facility
Federal Employee Health Plan - Use when known
BC Self-insured (ERISA) Administrative Services Only (ASO) Plan
Federal, State, Local not specified - not specified managed care
Medicare Managed Care (Includes Medicare Advantage Plans)
Local, not otherwise specified (other local, county)
Federal, State, Local not specified - HMO
Federal, State, Local not specified - FFS
Medicare Chronic Condition Special Needs Plan (C-SNP)
Federal, State, Local not specified - Other
Federal, State, Local not specified - POS
State SCHIP program (codes for individual states)
No Typology Code available for payment source
Indirect Care--Care provided outside VA facilities
Direct Care--Care provided in VA facilities
Self-pay (Includes applicants for insurance and Medicaid applicants)
Fully Integrated Dual Eligible Special Needs Plan (FIDE-SNP)
Other specified but not otherwise classifiable (includes Hospice - Unspecified plan)
Other Government (Federal, State, Local not specified)
Uniformed Services Family Health Plan (USFHP) -- HMO
NO PAYMENT from an Organization/Agency/Program/Private Payer Listed
Commercial Managed Care - Pharmacy Benefit Manager
Other Managed Care, Unknown if public or private
Managed Care (private) or private health insurance (indemnity), not otherwise specified
Civilian Health and Medical Program for the VA (CHAMPVA)
MANAGED CARE, UNSPECIFIED (to be used only if one can't distinguish public from private)
BC (Indemnity or Managed Care) - Out of State
Federal, State, Local not specified - PPO
Federal, State, Local not specified managed care
